title: A733 user manual SPC/TZMA deep audit
question: Does the public A733 user manual expose an SMHC0 security/firewall/SPC/TZMA bit that can explain the SDMMC0 IDMAC descriptor-read stall?
test_cost: low; manual text and local source audit only, no kernel build and no board reboot
observations: The memory map names SPC at 0x02054000, S_SPC at 0x07002000, and S_TZMA at 0x07003000.
The manual documents CCMU_SEC_SWITCH_REG and PRCM_SEC_SWITCH_REG security switches, but those govern clock-controller register accessibility, not SMHC0 IDMAC master memory access.
Searches found SMHC0 in clock/reset, boot-source, interrupt-vector, and SMHC register sections, but no SMHC0 SPC/TZMA permission bitfield or Linux-programmable firewall register.
The manual documents DMA controller channel security at DMA_SEC_REG, but SDMMC0 IDMAC is internal to SMHC0 and does not use the standalone DMA controller channel path.

decision.summary: The user manual supports the security/fabric suspicion only at a class level. It does not expose a source-backed SMHC0 SPC/TZMA/firewall bit or safe non-secure Linux programming path.
decision.next_step: Do not write SPC/TZMA registers. Keep the maintainer/vendor question focused on whether an undocumented secure-world handoff or SMHC0 master permission exists.
